title: What Word2Vec Proved
concept: Word2Vec proved that unsupervised training on large text corpora could learn rich semantic representations. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: GloVe & Embedding Math.'

exercises.prompt: What kind of data does Word2Vec learn from?
exercises.explain: Word2Vec is famous for proving that unsupervised training on raw text can build rich word embeddings.

cards.title: The Bridge to Modern AI
cards.body: Before Word2Vec, scientists thought we had to manually teach computers the definition of every single word. But Word2Vec proved that computers could teach themselves just by reading!

cards.title: The Big Breakthrough
cards.body: Word2Vec proved that we don't need labeled datasets to teach AI the meanings of words. By using 'unsupervised training' on massive piles of plain text, the AI automatically learns rich mathematical representations (embeddings) of what words mean based on their context.

cards.title: The Neighborhood Watch
cards.body: Think of Word2Vec like a person moving into a new town. By observing who sits next to whom at local cafes every day, they quickly figure out who belongs to the same friend groups, without ever asking for an official town directory!

cards.title: Learning Without Teachers
cards.body: Because Word2Vec reads millions of normal sentences without human help, it automatically notices that 'coffee' and 'tea' appear near words like 'drink', 'mug', and 'hot'. Even though nobody explained what breakfast is, the AI groups them together!

cards.title: The Labeled Data Myth
cards.body: People think AI needs perfect, human-labeled flashcards to learn language. In reality, Word2Vec proved that raw, unorganized books and articles are more than enough for an AI to learn rich word meanings on its own.

cards.title: Old vs. New Way
cards.body: See how Word2Vec changed the way we teach language to computers.

right.text: AI reading raw, unlabeled books to learn meanings automatically.

left.text: Humans manually tagging, labeling, and defining every word.

exercises.prompt: What did Word2Vec use to prove that computers can learn rich word meanings?

options.text: Unsupervised training on large text corpora
Human editors writing definitions for every word
Translating all words into binary code manually
Restricting the AI to only learn 100 words at a time

exercises.explain: Word2Vec proved that unsupervised training on large text corpora could learn rich semantic representations of words.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Word2Vec proved that AI can learn rich word meanings without humans labeling the data.
exercises.explain: Word2Vec proved that unsupervised training on raw text is incredibly powerful for learning word meanings.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about how Word2Vec learns.
exercises.sentence: Word2Vec proved that AI can learn word meanings using ___________ training on raw text.
exercises.explain: Unsupervised learning means the AI trains on raw text without humans labeling or organizing it first.

exercises.prompt: Which of these sentences looks like it was generated by an untrained, random AI rather than a real text corpus?

options.text: "The king sat on his golden throne."
"A warm cup of coffee starts the morning."
"Blue king runned very window fastly eat."

exercises.explain: AI generated this sentence. Real text from Word2Vec training would preserve natural semantic relationships, whereas this output is gibberish.

exercises.prompt: What was the ultimate breakthrough that Word2Vec proved to the world of AI?

options.text: It proved that AIs can only understand words if humans hand-write dictionary definitions for them.
It proved that AI can learn deep word meanings on its own, simply by reading huge amounts of raw text.
It proved that computers read text from right to left much faster than from left to right.
It proved that words have no real mathematical relationship to one another.

exercises.explain: Brilliant work! Word2Vec showed that we don't need human teachers to label every single word's meaning. Just by reading millions of sentences, an AI can figure out how words relate to each other. Next up: GloVe & Embedding Math.
